efficacyPairs.questionText: Which of these numbers is a prime number?
A maths challenge card asks: how many factors does 24 have?
Emma wants to arrange 18 chairs in equal rows with no chairs left over. Which of these is NOT a possible number of rows?
What is the smallest prime number?
Which number is both a factor of 30 and a factor of 45?
Jake has 36 chocolates. He wants to share them equally among his friends with none left over. Which number of friends is NOT possible?
A homework sheet asks pupils to list every prime number between 20 and 30. How many prime numbers are there between 20 and 30?
A teacher has 12 red pencils and 18 blue pencils. She wants to put them into equal groups with the same number of each colour. What is the largest gro
Which of these is NOT a prime number?
Sophie is making gift bags. She has 20 sweets and 30 stickers. She wants each bag to have the same number of sweets and the same number of stickers, w
Two flashing lights start together. One flashes every 4 seconds and the other every 6 seconds. After how many seconds will they next flash at the same
In class, the teacher writes 17 on the board and asks how many numbers divide into it exactly. How many factors does the number 17 have?
Tom says 'All odd numbers are prime'. Is he correct?
efficacyPairs.correctAnswer: No, because 9 is odd but not prime
